2605.19714.title: LLM-Based Financial Sentiment Analysis in Arabic: Evidence from Saudi Markets
2605.19714.paper_summary: Investor sentiment shapes financial markets, yet modeling sentiment in Arabic financial contexts remains challenging due to linguistic complexity and limited resources. We present an Arabic NLP framework for large-scale financial sentiment analysis tailored to the Saudi market, integrating official financial news and social media to capture institutional and public investor sentiment. The framework constructs a large Arabic financial corpus through a multi-stage pipeline encompassing data collection, cleaning, deduplication, entity linking, and sentiment annotation. Transformer-based NER combined with a curated company lexicon links textual mentions to canonical company identifiers, with sentiment labels assigned using a five-class scheme. The resulting dataset of 84K samples supports company-level sentiment aggregation and analysis of sentiment dynamics relative to stock market behavior on the Saudi Exchange. Experimental results demonstrate reliable and scalable Arabic financial sentiment analysis.
2605.19714.paper_summary_zh: 投資者情緒塑造金融市場，但在阿拉伯金融環境中建模情緒仍然具有挑戰性，這是由於語言的複雜性和資源的有限性。我們提出了一個針對沙烏地市場的大規模阿拉伯語自然語言處理框架，用於金融情緒分析，整合官方金融新聞和社交媒體，以捕捉機構和公眾投資者的情緒。該框架通過一個多階段的流程構建了一個大型阿拉伯金融語料庫，涵蓋數據收集、清理、去重、實體連結和情緒標註。基於Transformer的命名實體識別（NER）結合精心策劃的公司詞彙，將文本提及與標準公司標識符連結，情緒標籤則使用五類方案進行分配。最終生成的84K樣本數據集支持公司層面的情緒聚合和相對於沙烏地交易所股票市場行為的情緒動態分析。實驗結果顯示可靠且可擴展的阿拉伯金融情緒分析。
2605.19714.author: Mona H. Albaqawi et.al.
2605.19714.authors: Mona H. Albaqawi, Eman M. Albalkhi, Joud A. Albaiti, Enrico Lopedoto
